card.intuition: Two hooks used for memoization: `useMemo` caches the RESULT of an expensive calculation, while `useCallback` caches the FUNCTION instance itself.
card.analogy: A cheat sheet: `useMemo` is writing down the answer to '1423 * 54' (caching the result) so you don't have to do the math again. `useCallback` is keeping the same photocopy of the math steps (caching the function) so you don't have to write them out again every single time.
when_to_use: Preventing unnecessary re-renders of child components that use `React.memo` by providing stable function references
gotchas: Overusing them can actually SLOW DOWN your app because the overhead of tracking dependencies and storing the values isn't free
They only work if you provide the correct dependency array — if you miss a variable, you'll get a 'stale' value from the past

exercise.prompt: Identify which hook to use based on the goal.
exercise.test_code:
assert "Memo" in goals.values()
assert "Callback" in goals.values()
print("PASS")
